name: ALZ Compliance Ontology Glossary
description: Comprehensive glossary defining all entities, relationships, and terms used in the ALZ Compliance Ontology for Azure Landing Zone security assessments

entities.name: ComplianceFramework
entities.description: A regulatory or industry compliance framework that defines security and governance controls for cloud environments, such as MCSB, NIST 800-53, or ISO 27001
entities.usageExample: MCSB v2 framework containing 90+ security controls across 12 domains
entities.usageContext: Use when referencing a complete compliance standard that contains multiple controls
entities.businessMeaning: The regulatory standard an organization must comply with based on industry, geography, or contractual requirements
entities.technicalMeaning: A JSON-LD DefinedTermSet containing structured control definitions with implementation guidance and audit evidence requirements
entities.constraints: Must have unique frameworkId, version must follow semantic versioning
entities.aiAgentUsage: AI agents use frameworks to determine which controls apply to a given resource or subscription, and to calculate compliance scores

entities.name: ComplianceControl
entities.description: A specific security or governance requirement within a compliance framework that can be implemented and audited, such as NS-1 Network Segmentation or DP-3 Encryption in Transit
entities.usageExample: MCSB NS-1 requiring network segmentation between application tiers
entities.usageContext: Use when referencing a specific auditable requirement within a framework
entities.businessMeaning: A specific rule or requirement that must be satisfied to achieve compliance with a framework
entities.technicalMeaning: A DefinedTerm with controlId, controlFamily, and implementationGuidance properties defining what must be configured
entities.constraints: controlId must be unique within framework, description minimum 20 characters
entities.aiAgentUsage: AI agents evaluate controls against resource configurations to determine compliance status and generate findings

entities.name: ControlMapping
entities.description: A mapping relationship between equivalent controls across different compliance frameworks enabling cross-framework compliance assessment
entities.usageExample: MCSB NS-1 maps to NIST SC-7 with 'exact' mapping strength
entities.usageContext: Use when establishing equivalence between controls in different frameworks to reduce audit burden
entities.businessMeaning: Documentation that satisfying one control also satisfies equivalent controls in other frameworks
entities.technicalMeaning: A PropertyValue linking sourceControl to targetControl with mappingStrength enum (exact/partial/related)
entities.constraints: Must connect two different controls, mappingStrength required
entities.aiAgentUsage: AI agents use mappings to calculate unified compliance scores across multiple frameworks and identify coverage gaps

entities.name: AzureResource
entities.description: An Azure cloud resource that can be configured and audited for compliance with security controls, such as a Storage Account, Key Vault, or Virtual Network
entities.usageExample: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts resource in subscription with TLS 1.2 enabled
entities.usageContext: Use when referencing any Azure resource that needs compliance evaluation
entities.businessMeaning: A deployed cloud service or component that must meet security and governance requirements
entities.technicalMeaning: An Azure Resource Manager resource with resourceId, resourceType, resourceProvider, and configuration properties
entities.constraints: resourceId must be valid ARM resource ID format, resourceType from Azure provider namespace
entities.aiAgentUsage: AI agents query Resource Graph for resource inventory and evaluate configurations against control requirements

entities.name: ControlImplementation
entities.description: The specific Azure configuration or policy that implements a compliance control for a resource type
entities.usageExample: Azure Policy requiring TLS 1.2 minimum on Storage Accounts implements MCSB DP-3
entities.usageContext: Use when documenting how a control is technically implemented in Azure
entities.businessMeaning: The technical mechanism that enforces or validates a compliance requirement
entities.technicalMeaning: An Action with azurePolicyId, configurationProperty, expectedValue, and remediationAction
entities.constraints: Must reference valid Azure Policy definition ID or configuration property path
entities.aiAgentUsage: AI agents use implementations to determine expected configurations and identify non-compliant resources

entities.name: ComplianceAssessment
entities.description: A point-in-time evaluation of an Azure environment against compliance controls producing a compliance score and findings
entities.usageExample: Pre-ALZ audit assessing MCSB v1 and v2 compliance across 3 subscriptions
entities.usageContext: Use when performing a compliance evaluation of an Azure environment
entities.businessMeaning: A formal evaluation of security posture against compliance requirements at a specific point in time
entities.technicalMeaning: A Dataset containing assessmentDate, assessmentScope (subscription IDs), frameworksAssessed, overallScore, and findingsCount
entities.constraints: Must cover at least one framework, overallScore 0-100, findingsCount non-negative integer
entities.aiAgentUsage: AI agents orchestrate assessments by running KQL queries, evaluating resources, and calculating compliance scores

entities.name: ComplianceFinding
entities.description: A specific non-compliance issue identified during assessment with severity and remediation guidance
entities.usageExample: Storage Account lacking TLS 1.2 enforcement violating MCSB DP-3 with High severity
entities.usageContext: Use when documenting a specific compliance gap that requires remediation
entities.businessMeaning: A documented instance where a resource fails to meet a compliance requirement
entities.technicalMeaning: A Report with findingSeverity (critical/high/medium/low/informational), currentState, expectedState, and remediationGuidance
entities.constraints: findingSeverity required from enum, remediationPriority 1-5 integer
entities.aiAgentUsage: AI agents generate findings by comparing resource configurations against control requirements and prioritize remediation

entities.name: InsuranceSectorRequirement
entities.description: A regulatory requirement specific to the insurance sector that must be addressed through cloud compliance controls
entities.usageExample: FCA SYSC 13.9 requiring firms to ensure IT security for outsourced services
entities.usageContext: Use when mapping insurance-specific regulations to technical controls
entities.businessMeaning: A legal or regulatory obligation specific to insurance firms that drives compliance requirements
entities.technicalMeaning: A GovernmentService with regulatoryBody (FCA/PRA/Lloyd's/EIOPA/ICO), applicableRegulation, and enforcementDate
entities.constraints: regulatoryBody must be from defined enum, requirementId unique
entities.aiAgentUsage: AI agents map sector requirements to technical controls to ensure regulatory coverage and identify gaps

relationships.name: frameworkContainsControl
relationships.description: A compliance framework contains multiple compliance controls as its constituent requirements
relationships.aiAgentUsage: Agents traverse this relationship to enumerate all controls in a framework for assessment

relationships.name: controlBelongsToFramework
relationships.description: A compliance control belongs to a specific compliance framework

relationships.name: controlMapsTo
relationships.description: A compliance control maps to an equivalent control in another framework via a control mapping
relationships.aiAgentUsage: Agents use mappings to calculate multi-framework compliance scores

relationships.name: mappingConnectsControls
relationships.description: A control mapping connects a source control to a target control across frameworks

relationships.name: controlImplementedBy
relationships.description: A compliance control is implemented by a specific Azure configuration or policy

relationships.name: implementationSatisfiesControl
relationships.description: A control implementation satisfies the requirements of a compliance control

relationships.name: implementationAppliesToResource
relationships.description: A control implementation applies to a specific type of Azure resource

relationships.name: assessmentCoversFramework
relationships.description: A compliance assessment covers and evaluates against a compliance framework

relationships.name: resourceEvaluatedInAssessment
relationships.description: An Azure resource is evaluated as part of a compliance assessment

relationships.name: assessmentProducesFinding
relationships.description: A compliance assessment produces compliance findings as results

relationships.name: findingFromAssessment
relationships.description: A compliance finding results from a specific assessment

relationships.name: findingViolatesControl
relationships.description: A compliance finding indicates violation of a specific compliance control

relationships.name: findingAffectsResource
relationships.description: A compliance finding affects a specific Azure resource

relationships.name: sectorRequirementAddressedByControl
relationships.description: An insurance sector requirement is addressed by compliance controls

relationships.name: controlAddressesSectorRequirement
relationships.description: A compliance control addresses an insurance sector regulatory requirement

businessRules.name: Mandatory MCSB Assessment
businessRules.description: All ALZ implementations must be assessed against both MCSB v1 and MCSB v2
businessRules.ruleFormat: IF AzureLandingZone deployed THEN ComplianceAssessment MUST cover MCSB_v1 AND MCSB_v2

businessRules.name: Insurance Sector Compliance
businessRules.description: Insurance advisory workloads must address FCA and PRA requirements

businessRules.name: Critical Finding Remediation
businessRules.description: Critical severity findings must have high priority remediation

businessRules.name: Control Implementation Coverage
businessRules.description: Every compliance control must have at least one implementation

businessRules.name: Framework Version Requirement
businessRules.description: Compliance frameworks must follow semantic versioning
businessRules.ruleFormat: IF ComplianceFramework exists THEN frameworkVersion MUST match semver pattern

qualityMetrics.description: Quality metrics for the ALZ Compliance Glossary measuring coverage, completeness, and OAA v6.1.0 gate compliance status

changeControl.changeReason: Upgraded to OAA v6.1.0 compliance format with entities array, relationships with cardinality, and business rules

versionHistory.changes: Initial glossary creation with entity and relationship terms
Upgraded to OAA v6.1.0: added entities array, relationships with domainIncludes/rangeIncludes/cardinality, businessRules with IF-THEN format, qualityMetrics description
